content:
Playwright Chronicles: Crafting Elegant Automation in JS/TS from Scratch — Part 36: Playwright Config Secrets with Viewports, Devices, SSL & Location Testing

5 min read
Just now

--

In the 35th edition (Playwright Chronicles: Crafting Elegant Automation in JS/TS from Scratch — Part 35: The Power of Playwright Settings by Streamlining Your Testing Workflow with Ease | by Suresh Madhusanka Rodrigo | AI & QA Nexus | Jul, 2026 | Medium) of this series, we introduced the Playwright configuration file, the central hub for defining automation behavior. We explored its basic purpose, how to create separate configuration files beyond the default one, and how to execute tests across multiple projects defined within a single file.
Now, in the 36th edition, we build on those foundations by diving deeper into advanced features that make the configuration file even more powerful. These enhancements allow us to simulate real‑world scenarios, handle browser quirks and streamline responsive testing.
It will cover the below points.
- Viewport usage: Lets you simulate different screen sizes so you can check how your application responds across desktop, tablet and mobile layouts.
- Device mode: Enables testing on specific devices like iPhones, Pixels or Galaxy phones, replicating their environment…
